description:
Goal: make cross-module callable visibility a first-class compiler2 fact and collapse compiler2 onto function identities all the way down. `ModuleDefined(module)` will continue to mean the module body has been scoped/published; a new `ModuleInterface(module)` fact will mean the module's exported callable surface is available.

Strategy: replace `ModuleSurface.exports`, `World.module_exports`, and string-shaped export/callee residue with a module-owned callable interface built around `FunctionId`-backed entries, exact obligation tracking with requester provenance, and `Ready|Pending` set selection for `All`/`Except` import and require forms. Protocol modules should ride this same callable surface instead of remaining special, and later compiler tiers should stop carrying `Named` callees or named function refs once interface-backed resolution exists.

Signal: local, runtime, external, and protocol modules all publish one interface shape; import/require/remote lookup consume that interface; semantic/artifact/native tiers no longer carry unresolved named callable placeholders; missing-export diagnostics come from interface settlement with real requester spans; compiler2 no longer duplicates export selection or protocol-name specialness across callers.
title: Compiler2 callable interfaces: module-owned visibility, function identities, and external import resolution
